title: Restore failed with foreigh key table when ddl-batch-size=1
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)
1. create two tables, one has fk.
```
CREATE TABLE parent (
    id INT KEY
);

CREATE TABLE child (
    id INT,
    pid INT,
    INDEX idx_pid (pid),
    FOREIGN KEY (pid) REFERENCES parent(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
```
2. insert values into two tables
3. Do a full backup 
4. Do a full restore to another cluster with  `--ddl-batch-size=1`
<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
1. restore success

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
2. restore stuck

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
v6.5 or later(include master)
<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE parent (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE child (id INT PRIMARY KEY, pid INT, age INT CHECK (age >= 0), FOREIGN KEY (pid) REFERENCES parent(id) ON DELETE CASCADE);
INSERT INTO child (id, pid, age) VALUES (1, 1, 10), (2, 2, NULL), (3, NULL, 5);
SELECT * FROM child WHERE pid IS NULL OR age < 5;
key_bug_features: Restore with ddl-batch-size=1 on foreign key table
